The framing is genuinely novel (no cited work ties off-ball space ownership to a shot's realized StatsBomb xG plus human labels), but it fails feasibility on four repo-verified grounds: PFF tracking covers only 44 of 64 matches (research/data/frame_vaep_cache has 44 npz, CLAUDE.md confirms), so ~20 match-worth of shots have no build-up frames; PFF createsSpace is an ON-BALL actor tag (csPlayerId off-ball field populated ~2/1422 per research/scripts/eda_space_leaderboards.py), so it cannot validate the OFF-BALL space-owner the metric credits; the rho=0.88 anchor in research/notes/xg_grounding_findings.md is a team-tournament TOTAL (inflated by games-played; per-match rho=0.33 "NO-GO", per-match-rate 0.45) that explicitly does not support the proposed per-shot/per-player backward attribution; and no PFF-to-StatsBomb frame-level time alignment exists in the codebase (only a coarse team-match join).

combo.name: Self-Made Space (SMS): Receiving-Space Self-Generation Index
components: Next-receiver head (possessor-conditioned P(receive) per teammate — 'who gets open for THIS carrier', already trained, beats the ~40-44% nearest-teammate baseline at ~70% top-1) — the OPENNESS axis.

Fernandez & Bornn pitch-control / Space-Occupation surface (bivariate-normal player-influence field on a single frame, parametric, no training) — the SPACE axis: how much controllable receiving space the player occupies at the moment the carrier looks up.

Motion-forecast head as the built-in 2s average-player GHOST: SMS credits only the openness/space the player generated ABOVE what an average player at the same start state would have drifted into — isolating self-generated space from positional luck (C-OBSO-style reference, but reusing our trained ghost rather than a separate GVRNN).

PFF betterOptionPlayerId (518 'open-man-ignored' tags) as the PRIMARY ground-truth validator: a player flagged betterOption demonstrably manufactured receiving space the carrier failed to use — exactly the SMS construct.
PFF movementGrade (368, movementPlayerId) + positionGrade (1,300, positionPlayerId) as the secondary off-ball-quality validator (expert-graded run/positioning on the SAME off-ball mover).

PFF createsSpace (1,422) + StatsBomb real xG as TEAM/possession-level co-validation only (createsSpace attributes to the passer, so it checks the carrier-side completion of the SMS event, not the receiver).
combo.measures: SMS scores each off-ball ATTACKER, per frame and aggregated, on how much of their OWN future-receiving space they manufacture: SMS_t = (next-receiver P(receive) for that player, given the current carrier) x (Fernandez-Bornn space-occupation gain that player controls in the danger-weighted receiving zone), with the motion-forecast ghost subtracted so only space generated ABOVE the average-player drift counts. It answers a question no prior metric isolates: not 'who creates space for teammates' (SOG/SGG, C-OBSO) and not 'who is found by a pass' (xT, createsSpace=passer), but 'who becomes the open, controllable receiving option for the player on the ball — through their own movement.' Aggregated to a player leaderboard it ranks self-service space generators; the gap between SMS and actual receptions surfaces players who repeatedly get open and are ignored (the betterOption population).
combo.novelty: Every verified method splits cleanly on one side of SMS. xT and createsSpace value the on-ball/passer side only. SOG/SGG and C-OBSO measure space a player creates FOR TEAMMATES or for the team surface, with no model of whether THAT player is the intended/likely receiver — they are carrier- or surface-centric. Martens et al. SG_rec ('space for self') is the nearest cousin but is a pure pitch-control reconstruction with NO openness/affinity term — it does not condition on the actual ball-carrier's passing tendencies, so it cannot distinguish 'space a defender lets you have in a useless zone' from 'space the carrier can and will exploit.' SMS is the first to MULTIPLY a learned possessor-conditioned P(receive) (who this carrier targets) BY a continuous space-occupation surface (how much receiving space you control), then ghost-subtract an average-player baseline — fusing the receiver-affinity axis with the space axis. And it is the only one with a true off-ball-RECEIVER ground truth (betterOption + movement/position grades on the mover), where the entire literature is validated only by expert video review (Fernandez-Bornn, C-OBSO) or has no ground truth at all.
combo.feasibility_note: Feasible: the next-receiver head (output/transformer + scripts/train_next_receiver.py), the motion-forecast/ghost (data/future_motion.py), and a single-frame Fernandez-Bornn influence surface are all in-repo, and betterOptionPlayerId/movementGrade/positionGrade resolve cleanly to the off-ball mover (per research/scripts/eda_space_leaderboards.py) — the one honest caveat to state on screen is that PFF createsSpace attributes to the PASSER, not the open receiver, so SMS must be validated against betterOption + movement/position grades (true receiver labels) with createsSpace used only as a team/possession-level co-check, and SMS is only trustworthy on-camera (~46% of off-ball positions are imputed, so off-camera runs are excluded).
combo.visual_type: leaderboard (SMS player ranking) with a linked interactive play scrubber: each top entry deep-links to a clip where the next-receiver P(receive) halo lights up on the off-ball mover AS their Fernandez-Bornn space-occupation surface inflates and the average-player ghost stays behind, with betterOption frames flagged as ground-truth confirmation.
combo.youtube_segment: "The most open man you never noticed: Pedri keeps manufacturing his own receiving space — leading SMS while topping the league at 69.5 pressured actions/match — and the betterOption tags prove teammates kept missing him; SMS is how we'll spot the next one before 2026."

verdicts.reason: SMS is feasible (next-receiver head in model/next_receiver.py, motion-forecast ghost in data/future_motion.py, and PFF betterOption/movement/position labels all resolve cleanly per eda_space_leaderboards.py), but it is not novel: it merely re-bundles three already-enumerated in-house ideas — C2 "The Most Ignored Open Man" (next-receiver x betterOptionPlayerId), C1 "Space Above Replacement" (the ghost-subtraction, which the doc itself calls C-OBSO redux), and B2/C4 (Fernandez-Bornn surface validated by labels) — while its one genuine delta over Martens SG_rec (multiplying by a learned receiver-affinity) leans on a next-receiver signal the repo already found is rho=0.885 redundant with the recorded passing network, and its "primary ground truth" (518 sparse passer-error betterOption tags plus near-entirely-negative deduction grades) is too thin and indirect to validate a per-frame self-generated-space index.

SMS survives as a novel, buildable on-camera/44-match deliverable, but only after correcting three overstatements I verified against the repo: the Fernandez-Bornn surface is NOT in-repo (must be built; it's a standard parametric single-frame method, so still feasible), the metric engine covers 44 tracking matches not "all 64" (only 44 PFF tracking files exist and the cache/default_split confirm 36+8=44), and the primary betterOption validator yields only 335 usable tags inside the tracking subset (518 total; 183 are event-only), with movement 253/368 and position 928/1300 in-tracking. Novelty holds: the next-receiver head is genuinely trained (val_top1=0.701 vs 0.439 nearest baseline, exactly as claimed) and the literature splits cleanly — no verified method multiplies a learned possessor-conditioned P(receive) by a space-occupation surface then ghost-subtracts an average-player drift, and betterOption is a real receiver-side ground truth absent from the entire (video-validated) prior art. The most serious threat is off-camera censoring: I measured 50% of player-observations as ESTIMATED, and off-ball runners getting open are exactly the population most likely off-camera, so the (honestly disclosed, visibility-flag-enabled) on-camera restriction systematically excludes many of the moments SMS exists to detect — survivable but it caps this at an exploratory on-camera metric, not the tournament-complete claim the framing implies.

combo.name: TURN — Turned Under-line Reception Network (the half-turn-behind-lines metric)
components: PFF human label receiverFacingType (G=facing goal / B=facing own goal / L=lateral) — the receiver-orientation-at-reception signal, populated on ~83% of passes (8,232 G of 21,005 over 19 matches), DENSER than the null bodyMovementType the theme named; it is the operational stand-in for bodyMovementType/orientation

PFF human label linesBrokenType (D=defensive line / M=midfield / A=attacking line + combos AM/MD/AMD) — the expert tag naming WHICH tactical line the pass broke, i.e. the 'behind-lines' / 'receptions-behind-lines' component
PFF passOutcomeType=='C' + open-play filter (setpieceType 'O') gating to genuine completed receptions; receiver attributed via receiverPlayerId

Transformer next-receiver head (val top-1 70.0%, top-3 94.6%, beats nearest-baseline 43.9%; output/training_metrics_nextrcvr_main.json) — to weight each reception by P(this player gets open) so unrewarded turns still credit the run

Transformer xT-regression head (off-ball xT LIFT over the static lookup; prototyped in scripts/analyze_space_and_chemistry.py, output/space_attribution.json) AND calibrated P(score) delta — to VALUE each turned-under-line reception rather than merely count it
StatsBomb REAL xG (all 64 matches; research/scripts/compute_xg_analytics.py joining statsbomb_xg per shot) — to cash the metric out into actual chance quality in the possession that follows the turn

Geometric line-break reconstruction from event snapshots (research/scripts/eda_line_breaks.py, >=3 opponents bypassed in a forward corridor) as an independent cross-check on the PFF linesBrokenType tag
PFF createsSpace tags (1,422) + movementGrade/positionGrade as the GROUND-TRUTH validation set: report precision/recall of high-TURN receptions against expert space tags
combo.measures: TURN measures how often, and how valuably, a player RECEIVES the ball already turned toward goal on the far side of a defensive/midfield/attacking line — i.e. the on-the-half-turn forward reception that converts a line break into immediate forward threat instead of a sideways recycle. Each qualifying reception (completed, open-play, linesBrokenType populated, receiverFacingType=='G') is weighted by the transformer's off-ball xT-lift / ΔP(score) at that frame and credited downstream with the StatsBomb real xG generated in the ensuing possession; a next-receiver-head term also credits players who got turned-and-open even when the pass never came (fusing the betterOptionPlayerId 'ignored open man' idea). It is reported as a tournament leaderboard (count, value, and value-per-reception) and as a player pass-network of who-feeds-whom-on-the-turn. Empirically the signal is clean and sparse: of 1,490 completed line-break receptions over 19 matches only 265 (17.8%) arrive turned-to-goal — these are the high-value moments. Tournament leaders surface immediately (Bellingham, De Paul, Di Maria, Messi, plus the Croatia/Morocco/Argentina clusters), and in the 2022 final Di Maria alone produced 6+ turned-under-line receptions.
combo.novelty: Closes the single gap the EPV authors (Fernandez/Bornn/Cervone) named themselves — 'no body orientation' — but does it without inferring orientation from noisy broadcast tracking: it uses PFF's HUMAN-GRADED receiverFacingType, an orientation-at-reception label no public space metric has ever paired with tracking. xT (Singh) is on-ball and successful-only and explicitly ignores off-ball effects; OBSO (Spearman) ignores the toward-goal preference of the transition AND has no orientation; pitch-control/SOG-SGG (Fernandez-Bornn) and C-OBSO (Teranishi) are all position-only surfaces with NO facing term and no ground truth (validated by analysts watching video / by salary). Martens-Dick-Brefeld add per-player movement but no orientation and only 54 Bundesliga matches. TURN is the first metric to make 'received facing the right way' a first-class, expert-labelled, transformer-valued quantity, validated against independent PFF createsSpace/movement tags rather than two analysts eyeballing clips, at full tournament scale (all 64 matches).
combo.feasibility_note: Fully feasible today and largely event-snapshot-based (so robust to the ~46% off-camera imputation): receiverFacingType and linesBrokenType already exist and are densely populated in every PFF event file (verified: 8,232 'G' receptions and 2,040 line-breaking-tag passes across 19 matches), the next-receiver/xT-lift/P(score) heads are trained, and StatsBomb xG joins per shot — the only build work is the join script plus label-based validation, with the honest caveat that the transformer-valued (xT-lift/ΔP-score) layer inherits the broadcast-tracking occlusion while the count/leaderboard layer does not.
combo.visual_type: interactive play scrubber (the polished interactive-plays.js module): a turned-under-line reception clip with the broken line drawn across the pitch, a rotating receiver-orientation arrow flipping from lateral/back to GOAL at the moment of the touch, and the P(score) strip ticking up — backed by a tournament TURN leaderboard panel
combo.youtube_segment: "The Half-Turn That Won the World Cup": watch Angel Di Maria receive on the turn behind France's lines six times in the 2022 final (TURN leaderboard topper that night) — the orientation no other space metric can see, the move that bent Argentina's third star into existence.

verdicts.reason: Feasibility fully verified (receiverFacingType 82.9% populated, linesBrokenType D/M/A present, createsSpace=1,422 and betterOptionPlayerId=518 exact, Di Maria=6 turned-under-line receptions in the final reproduced, next-receiver head trained), but TURN is not novel: it is the verbatim realization of a pre-specified brainstorm theme (grand_unifying_theory.mjs line 103, "orientation × receptions-behind-lines... receiving on the half-turn to play forward; closes the EPV no-orientation gap"), and its only deltas from that theme are swapping the denser receiverFacingType label for bodyMovementType plus the same transformer-valuation/StatsBomb-xG/createsSpace-validation machinery the sibling composites all reuse, so the core fusion was handed to it rather than discovered.

The core premise is novel and its count layer is feasible (verified all 64 matches: receiverFacingType present on 99.3% of completed passes and linesBrokenType on 8.5%, with TURN-qualifying receptions = 812/18.1% of line breaks, corroborating the claim), but the composite as written overclaims on three load-bearing legs — the StatsBomb "ensuing-possession xG" credit is infeasible because the only PFF<->StatsBomb linkage (match_join.py) is match-level with no possession/temporal alignment; the geometric "independent cross-check" is contradicted by the project's own eda_line_breaks.py, which documents only ~21-30% overlap with linesBrokenType, attributes to the passer not the receiver, and states they "measure different things"; and the next-receiver phantom-turn term has no orientation signal since receiverFacingType only exists on completed passes.

combo.name: UNLOCK — Attention-Gated Space Generation (directed "who frees whom" off the ball)
components: Attention chemistry: ball-independent, DIRECTED pair attention p->q from the score-specialist transformer (encode_with_attention), reusing the extract_aw_joi.py per-frame ball/player attention machinery but reading the player-token p->q row instead of only ball->player, so coupling is asymmetric (who p is mentally tied to).

Counterfactual SG_pas (learned, not parametric): the off-ball space teammate q GAINS because of p's movement, computed via the existing whiteboard counterfactual head (compute_whiteboard_counterfactuals.py) -- freeze/replace player p with the motion-forecast head's 2s 'average ghost' (forecasting_head.py), then recompute (a) q's next-receiver P(receive | possessor) from the possessor-conditioned next_receiver head and (b) q's xT-regression off-ball lift surface (xt_regression.py). SG_pas(p->q) = sum_t [ P(receive_q | p actual) - P(receive_q | p ghosted) ] * xT_lift_q(t). This is space-for-a-SPECIFIC-teammate, ball-independent.
Fusion: UNLOCK(p->q) = sum over off-ball frames of attn_pq(t) * max(SG_pas(p->q, t), 0), accumulated tournament-wide like AW-JOI; directed (p unlocks q != q unlocks p).

Ground-truth validation layer: PFF human labels createsSpace (1,422 tags) and betterOptionPlayerId (518 open-man-ignored tags), extracted via eda_space_leaderboards.py -- the 'space ground truth' the field lacks. Precision/recall of high-UNLOCK(p->q) frames against createsSpace on p and betterOptionPlayerId pointing at q.

Heads/assets fused: score-specialist attention + motion-forecast (ghost) head + next-receiver head + xT-regression head + PFF createsSpace/betterOptionPlayerId labels (StatsBomb xG used only as a downstream r-with-xG sanity check on receivers, a la Martens SG_rec~xG r=0.66).
combo.measures: Per ordered pair (creator p, beneficiary q): the off-ball space p's movement generates SPECIFICALLY for q, gated by how strongly p and q are coupled in the model's ball-independent attention. High UNLOCK(p->q) = p habitually makes runs/occupies defenders that open q as a passing option even when neither touches the ball. Aggregated to a directed player-pair graph and to a per-player 'unlocker score' (out-strength) vs 'beneficiary score' (in-strength) -- separating the players who FREE others from the players who CASH IN the freedom, which xT and OBSO conflate.
combo.novelty: Martens/Dick/Brefeld SG_pas is UNDIRECTED (space for teammates collectively), PARAMETRIC (per-player pitch-control movement models), ball-coupled, and one Bundesliga club-season (54 matches, never tournament-scale). Fernandez & Bornn SGG is single-frame parametric with NO ground truth (two analysts on video). AW-JOI (our own prior art) is attention x on-ball Delta-P_score -- it scores the VALUE the ball moves through a pair, not the ball-INDEPENDENT space one player frees for another. C-OBSO ghosts a player's own OBSO, not the space they create for a named teammate. NONE produce a DIRECTED, ball-independent, learned (counterfactual-ghosted), tournament-scale 'who unlocks whom' surface, and none is validated against independent expert space labels. UNLOCK is the first: attention supplies direction, the counterfactual head supplies a learned SG_pas (no parametric pitch-control assumption), and PFF createsSpace/betterOptionPlayerId convert 'no ground truth for space' into a precision/recall number.
combo.feasibility_note: Every component already exists in-repo (score-specialist attention + motion-forecast, next-receiver, xT-regression heads, the whiteboard counterfactual loop, and the createsSpace/betterOptionPlayerId extractors), so UNLOCK is a new accumulation script reusing extract_aw_joi.py's frame loop -- the only real caveat is the ~46% off-camera-imputed broadcast positions, which must be flagged per-frame (reuse the occlusion audit) and the metric reported on camera-confident off-ball frames so a ghosted-vs-imputed run is not mistaken for created space.
combo.visual_type: Directed pass network / unlock graph: a team's players as nodes, arrows p->q weighted and colored by UNLOCK(p->q) (out-strength sizing the 'unlocker' nodes, in-strength the 'beneficiaries'), with a click-through to a ghosting animation that shows the play with p's run vs the average-ghost so the freed space for q is literally drawn on the pitch-control surface.
combo.youtube_segment: "Who frees Messi -- and who does Messi free?" Run UNLOCK on Argentina's title run: show that Messi is the tournament's top BENEFICIARY-unlocker hybrid but that a specific teammate (e.g. Mac Allister / Di Maria) has the highest UNLOCK->Messi arrow, with the ghosting clip proving Messi's 6.03 xG was partly manufactured by an off-ball runner the box score never credits, then validate the moment against a PFF createsSpace tag on screen.

verdicts.reason: The directed, ball-independent, learned "who-unlocks-whom" concept is novel vs the literature (Martens SG_pas is undirected/parametric, C-OBSO ghosts a player's own value, CHASE is defense-side, AW-JOI is on-ball), but the composite is not feasible as specified and its load-bearing novelty pillar is false: per the repo's own eda_space_leaderboards.py header, createsSpace tags the ON-BALL actor (the dedicated csPlayerId is usable on ~2 of 1,422 events) so it cannot validate that off-ball p created space, and betterOptionPlayerId is a single-player "open man missed" tag, not a directed (p,q) pair — so the "precision/recall against PFF space ground truth" that is the whole premise's differentiator cannot validate a directed p→q edge; additionally the xt_regression head emits one pooled scalar per frame (encoded.mean(dim=1)), not the per-teammate xT_lift_q(t) surface SG_pas requires, each head sits on a separately-trained backbone (score-specialist vs transformer_nextrcvr_main vs xt vs relational) rather than one model, the whiteboard counterfactual only does dx/dy position shifts with no ghost-substitution mechanism, and the asset is 44 matches on disk, not the claimed 64.

The directed ball-independent "who unlocks whom" concept is genuinely novel versus the cited prior art, but it fails on feasibility: its ground-truth validation is invalid (eda_space_leaderboards.py documents createsSpace as an ON-BALL passer attribute, not an off-ball-runner tag — the dedicated csPlayerId is populated 2/1422), the xT-regression head outputs one pooled scalar per frame and cannot produce the per-teammate xT_lift_q surface SG_pas requires, the whiteboard head only perturbs+reruns score/concede (it never ghosts via the motion-forecast head nor touches the next-receiver/xT heads, so the core pipeline is unbuilt and would require fusing 3+ separately-trained incompatible backbones), and the tracking substrate is 44 matches not the claimed 64 with ~46% off-camera imputation concentrated on exactly the off-ball runners the metric targets.

survivors: Cost of the Closed Eye (CoCE) — "The Ignored Open Man Index"
TURN — Turned Under-line Reception Network (the half-turn-behind-lines metric)

theory.thesis: Every method in the literature is computing one slice of the same object — the value of space — and they only look incompatible because each fixes a different free variable. xT freezes the pitch into a static ball-location grid; OBSO/EPV freeze time into a single snapshot and the player into an average; SOG/SGG/Martens freeze value out of the picture and measure geometric area; C-OBSO and our transformer ghost introduce time and a counterfactual but freeze identity; CHASE and line-breaks measure only the defensive reaction or the on-ball completion. Read together they form one continuous quantity: the expected goal-probability mass a team controls across the whole pitch, decomposed along five orthogonal axes — WHERE (ball-location threat, xT), WHO controls it (pitch-control/PPCF), HOW MUCH is created above a counterfactual (ghost-relative lift, C-OBSO/our motion-forecast), WHO it is created for vs by (SG_rec/SG_pas, next-receiver affinity), and WHAT the defense concedes in reaction (CHASE). The unifying object is a per-frame, per-player off-ball value surface — Transition × Control × Score integrated over the pitch and differenced against an average-player ghost — and the seven prior methods are exactly the special cases you recover by collapsing one axis at a time. Our tracking transformer is the first instrument that holds all five axes open at once (calibrated P(score), off-ball xT-lift Spearman 0.714 vs 0.616 lookup, next-receiver top-1 0.70 vs 0.44, a built-in 2s ghost, counterfactual ΔP, and ball-independent attention), and it reconstructs StatsBomb team xG at rho 0.885 — tighter than xG tracks goals (0.82). The theory is therefore not a new metric but a coordinate system in which all space metrics are projections, plus the one dataset (PFF human labels) that finally gives those projections a ground truth.
theory.shared_blind_spot: No prior method has ground truth for space. Every single one is validated either by nothing (xT, OBSO, line-breaks are self-defining), by an outcome proxy that fires too late and too rarely to see the off-ball movement (xG, which only exists at the shot instant; SG_rec correlates at r=0.66 with it), or — most tellingly — by "two analysts watched video" (Fernandez & Bornn 2018) and salary correlation (C-OBSO, rho=0.45). The construct they all model — "space" — is unobservable, so they each substitute their own geometry for truth and then cannot tell a value-laden run from a decoy in a dead zone, an open man found from one ignored, or an elite mover from his average replacement. The blind spot is not a missing feature; it is the absence of any frame-level human label of WHEN space was actually created and WHO it was created for. PFF's 1,422 createsSpace tags, 518 betterOptionPlayerId tags, and the movement/position grades — paired with 30Hz tracking on all 64 matches — are the first frame-aligned space ground truth in the field, and no one else has it.
theory.pff_substrate: The PFF human labels are the keystone that turns five disconnected geometries into one validated theory, because each label is the missing external check for a specific axis. createsSpace (1,422 expert-tagged moments, attributed to the on-ball actor who played the ball into space) is the positive ground truth for the WHERE/HOW-MUCH axes: a credible OBSO/SOG/xT-lift surface must spike at or just before these frames and stay flat elsewhere — converting "two analysts watched video" into precision/recall on a 64-match benchmark. betterOptionPlayerId (518 open-man-ignored tags) is the unique validator for the WHO-it's-for axis: it is the only label in existence that names a teammate who demonstrably got open and was not found, so it grounds the next-receiver head and the entire EPV "value of every teammate" surface against a human-flagged correct answer. pressureType (N/P/A/L on ~62% of carriers) supplies the defensive-pressure-on-carrier signal Spearman explicitly named OBSO as ignoring, as an independently graded gate rather than a re-derived one. movementGrade (368, ~71% negative) and positionGrade (1,300, ~99% negative) are deduction-only off-ball-quality grades that calibrate the ghost-gap and let "least-bad" be ranked against a metric that credits good movement. receiverFacingType (G/B/L, ~83% of passes) / bodyMovementType supply the body-orientation channel EPV named as its other gap. Critically, these labels validate but cannot train a continuous surface (sparse, ~27 createsSpace/match, selection-biased to on-camera high-leverage moments) — so the transformer trains on dense tracking and is graded against the sparse labels. Internal coherence confirms they are real signal, not noise: the createsSpace leaderboard independently recovers known-elite movers (Messi #1 at 25, 23 under pressure; Lozano 5.67/match) and team totals (Croatia and Argentina lead) without being told who they are.

key_edges.fix: OBSO replaces xT's single ball-location lookup with a pitch-wide integral of Transition x Control x Score, valuing off-ball positions xT cannot see.
SOG/SGG supplies the continuous off-ball space axis xT lacks (e.g. Messi's 66.7%-passive space gain that xT scores as zero).
Both off-ball/unexecuted moments produce zero xT, exposing and grounding exactly the successful-on-ball-only blind spot (our xT-lift head: Spearman 0.616 -> 0.714).

families.family: Pitch control / space surfaces (WHO controls it + HOW MUCH)

key_edges.fix: Gating OBSO's Transition term by graded carrier pressure restores the defensive-pressure-on-carrier signal Spearman named as ignored.
createsSpace replaces 'two analysts watched video' with a 64-match label benchmark; multiplying the space surface by real xG turns area-won into space that produced shot quality.

The transformer's 2s ghost is a tournament-scale, all-player learned movement model replacing Martens' 54-match single-club fit, and betterOptionPlayerId validates his never-checked SG_pas teammate-serving half.

families.family: Possession value with a counterfactual (HOW MUCH above a ghost + identity)

key_edges.fix: Orientation labels add the body-facing channel EPV named missing (the Rakitic example), up-weighting a teammate on the half-turn to play forward.
Validates EPV's un-checked per-teammate off-ball value surface: the high-EPV off-ball teammate should be the human-flagged ignored 'better option'.
The transformer's all-player 2s ghost replaces Teranishi's single-season 3-player GVRNN, recomputing ghost-relative ΔP(score) at tournament scale.

families.family: Defensive geometry / penetration (WHAT the defense concedes)

key_edges.fix: Weighting each unit of hull deformation by ΔP(concede) converts geometric reaction into conceded value, killing the decoy-in-a-dead-zone false positive; positionGrade (~99% negative) is the defender ground truth.
Reassigns credit from the on-ball passer to the off-ball runner whose run opened the corridor, splitting the line break between passer and mover.
The expert tactical-line tag cross-checks the geometry-only corridor proxy (~30% overlap quantifies divergence and bounds the best threshold).

families.family: The unifying instrument (all five axes held open)
nodes: Tracking transformer heads (ghosting forecast, next-receiver, P(score), xT-lift, counterfactual, attention)

key_edges.fix: Convert the internally-validated xT-lift/ghost-gap surfaces into label-validated metrics via precision/recall against expert space-creation and movement tags.
The next-receiver head's rising P(receive) for a teammate should coincide with the 518 frames analysts flagged an open man as the ignored better option — grounding 'who gets open' as a skill.

Accumulated possession value reconstructs StatsBomb team-tournament xG at rho 0.885 (tighter than xG-to-goals 0.82); defensive chemistry predicts xGA beyond FIFA+games (partial -0.355, 90% CI excludes 0).

headline_metric.name: SAR — Space Above Replacement (pressure-gated, ghost-relative, label-grounded off-ball value)
headline_metric.recipe: For every off-ball attacker, every frame: (1) compute the OBSO-style off-ball value of their location as Transition x Control(PPCF) x Score, but replace Spearman's distance-only Score with the transformer's calibrated P(score) head (multi-defender/goal-angle-aware, learned) and sharpen the Transition term with the next-receiver head's possessor-conditioned P(receive) (who THIS carrier can actually reach, top-1 0.70 vs 0.44 nearest baseline); (2) multiply the Transition (deliverability) term by a learned gate keyed to the PFF-graded carrier pressureType (N/P/A/L) and carrier speed — so a run is only credited with danger that is passable given how hard the carrier is hassled, closing the exact gap Spearman named; (3) subtract the SAME quantity computed for the transformer's 2s motion-forecast GHOST (the all-player, tournament-scale average-player counterfactual) so only space generated ABOVE an average mover counts — SAR is the lift. Report SAR per-90 as a leaderboard and as actual-minus-ghost ΔP(score) via the whiteboard counterfactual machinery, cashed into StatsBomb-xG-equivalent terms (team-tournament rho 0.885). VALIDATE, not train, against PFF createsSpace (1,422) for precision/recall, betterOptionPlayerId (518) for the ignored-receiver tail, and movement/positionGrade for run quality.
headline_metric.why_novel: It is the first metric to hold all five axes of the space-value object open simultaneously and the first with frame-level human ground truth. C-OBSO added a ghost and goal-angle Score but left Spearman's pressure/carrier-speed Transition gap untouched, ran on one club season / 3 players, and had no ground truth (validated only by salary rho=0.45). SOG/SGG and EPV are average-player, single-frame, and unvalidated by anything but expert video. SAR closes Spearman's named transition-pressure gap using a real independently-human-graded pressure label rather than re-derived pressure, replaces the average Score model with a learned calibrated P(score), differences against an all-player tournament-scale ghost instead of a 3-player GVRNN, and — uniquely — grades the result against 1,422 createsSpace and 518 betterOption tags. It is C-OBSO with the one term C-OBSO never repaired, at 64-match scale, finally validated against humans instead of salary or video.

youtube_segments.title: The metric that doesn't exist: nobody can measure space
youtube_segments.metric: The field-wide no-ground-truth problem (createsSpace 1,422 / betterOption 518 as the first frame-level space labels)
youtube_segments.visual: Split screen: Fernandez & Bornn's 2018 caption 'validated by two analysts watching video' next to PFF's 1,422 expert createsSpace tags overlaid on a pitch — the moment a soft concept gets a label.
youtube_segments.star_example: Messi: the #1 space creator in Qatar with 25 expert-tagged creations (23 of them under pressure) — yet xT scores most of that movement as exactly zero because he never touched the ball.
youtube_segments.aha: For 50 years 'space' was a thing pundits pointed at and analysts eyeballed; the entire analytics literature validated it with video and salaries. PFF's human labels are the first time space has a ground truth — and that one fact reorganizes every metric below it.

youtube_segments.title: One object, five shadows: the unifying coordinate system
youtube_segments.metric: The space-value object = Transition x Control x Score, differenced against a ghost (xT, OBSO, SOG, EPV, C-OBSO are projections of it)
youtube_segments.visual: A rotating 3D value surface over the pitch; as it spins, each prior metric flickers in as the flat shadow you get by collapsing one axis (freeze the pitch -> xT; freeze time -> OBSO; drop value -> SOG; drop identity -> C-OBSO ghost).
youtube_segments.star_example: Our xT-lift head: a static ball-location grid scores 0.616 Spearman against real threat; add the off-ball tracking surface and it jumps to 0.714 — the +0.10 is literally the off-ball space xT was blind to.
youtube_segments.aha: These methods were never rivals. They are five photographs of the same statue from five angles — and once you see the statue, you know exactly which photo is missing which limb.

youtube_segments.title: The run that only counts when you're getting kicked
youtube_segments.metric: Pressure-gated OBSO / SAR Transition term (gated by PFF pressureType N/P/A/L on ~62% of carriers)
youtube_segments.visual: Interactive play scrubber under a live clip with a pressure DIAL on the carrier; drag N->P->A->L and watch every team's off-ball danger surface deflate — except the resilient ones.
youtube_segments.star_example: Messi tops actions-under-pressure at 472 (67.4/match, #1) AND space-creations-under-pressure at 23 (#1); drag the dial to a lunge and his open men stay reachable while others' collapse.
youtube_segments.aha: Spearman himself wrote that OBSO 'ignores defensive pressure and carrier speed.' The space that survives a lunge is a different, rarer skill than space in acres of time — and it's the space that wins knockout games.

youtube_segments.title: The most ignored man in Qatar
youtube_segments.metric: CoCE / Ignored-Open-Man cost (betterOptionPlayerId 518 tags x next-receiver P(receive) x whiteboard ΔP(score))
youtube_segments.visual: Dual leaderboard — 'Closed Eye' (passers who left value on the table) and 'Ignored Creator' (movers who got open and weren't found); each row expands to a clip with a ghost ball flying to the open man and a live ΔP(score) readout.
youtube_segments.star_example: Youssef En-Nesyri got open and was overlooked 8 times — most at the World Cup; Argentina ignored open men 32 times, tournament-most, even on the way to the trophy.
youtube_segments.aha: Every method values the open man only in the abstract. This is the first metric anchored to a human tag of WHICH open man was actually missed — putting a goal-probability price on a pass that was never played.

youtube_segments.title: The half-turn that bent the third star into existence
youtube_segments.metric: TURN — turned-under-line receptions (receiverFacingType=G x linesBrokenType x xT-lift), the orientation EPV said it couldn't see
youtube_segments.visual: Scrubber with the broken defensive line drawn across the pitch and a receiver-orientation arrow that flips from lateral/back to GOAL at the touch, P(score) strip ticking up.
youtube_segments.star_example: In the 2022 final Angel Di Maria received on the turn behind France's lines 6+ times — that night Argentina broke France's lines 46 to 16, mirroring the 2.76-2.27 xG edge.
youtube_segments.aha: EPV's authors named 'no body orientation' as their gap and used the Rakitic example. PFF graded receiver facing on 83% of passes — so we can finally separate the sideways recycle from the spin that turns a line break into a shot.

youtube_segments.title: Whose legs? Replacing the average player
youtube_segments.metric: SAR ghost-gap with player identity (motion-forecast 2s ghost vs actual; Martens SG_rec individualized movement, r=0.66 with xG)
youtube_segments.visual: Two trajectories over one clip: a translucent average-player GHOST drifting where a generic player would, and the real star pulling away into space the ghost never reaches — the gap shaded as SAR.
youtube_segments.star_example: Line-break leaders are ball-playing CBs and #6s — Stones 37, Modric 36, Rodri 35, Gvardiol 35, Brozovic 34, Maguire 33 — with Messi (33) the ONLY forward in the top 7; the ghost-gap is where his profile diverges from theirs.
youtube_segments.aha: An average-player model under-credits the players whose average IS elite — the Messi-effect EPV and C-OBSO both named. Subtracting a ghost isolates skill above replacement, the first honest answer to 'whose legs made that space?'

youtube_segments.title: Does the defense agree it lost? Gravity and the conceded hull
youtube_segments.metric: CHASE convex-hull deformation x transformer P(concede) x PFF positionGrade (~99% negative defender deductions)
youtube_segments.visual: The defensive convex hull collapsing/displacing as an attacker drifts, shaded by ΔP(concede) so you see whether the dragged defenders were guarding a dangerous zone or chasing a decoy.
youtube_segments.star_example: Croatia (232) and Argentina (212) led team line breaks AND space-creation; their opponents' hulls should deform most — and the defenders graded out of position (positionGrade) should be the ones the gravity pulled.
youtube_segments.aha: Attacker-side surfaces measure space taken; CHASE measures space conceded. Pair them and a decoy in a dead zone scores zero while a run that genuinely breaks the shape scores — the defense's own geometry becomes the second witness.

youtube_segments.title: Proof it's real: from space to a goal you can count
youtube_segments.metric: Accumulated transformer value vs StatsBomb real xG (team-tournament rho 0.885; defensive chemistry partial -0.355 beyond FIFA+games)
youtube_segments.visual: Scatter of every team's accumulated model value vs StatsBomb xG hugging the diagonal at rho 0.885, annotated 'tighter than xG tracks goals (0.82)'; then 2026 nations sliding onto the same plot as a teaser.
youtube_segments.star_example: The 2022 final: ARG 2.76 - 2.27 FRA xG with a 46-16 line-break split — the space story and the outcome story are the same story, and the model reconstructs it from movement alone.
youtube_segments.aha: The whole framework grounds out in a number you can verify against goals — so when we point the same instrument at the 2026 World Cup, the space leaderboard isn't a vibe, it's a calibrated forecast of who is manufacturing chances before the ball ever arrives.

open_risks: Broadcast-tracking occlusion is the dominant threat: ~46% of off-ball positions are imputed (~7m error, Penn et al. 2025), and they are imputed precisely for the off-camera off-ball players whose runs we most want to measure — so the pitch-control/PPCF surface, the ghost, and CHASE's defender hull are all degraded exactly where space creation happens. Every off-ball claim must carry a per-event visibility gate, and 'getting open off-camera' is fundamentally unreliable; the event-snapshot layers (line-breaks, TURN counts) are robust, the continuous/tracking-valued layers are not.

Identity blindness is unsolved, not solved: the transformer token has no player embedding and is trained on a single 44-match tournament, so the ghost is an average-player ghost. SAR's 'space above replacement' systematically under-credits stars whose average IS elite — the same Messi-effect/scale wall EPV named and Martens hit at 54 matches. We can name the gap but a single World Cup is far too little to learn player-specific movement models; identity embeddings are aspirational.

Label attribution is a known confound: createsSpace is attributed to the ON-BALL actor who played the ball into space, NOT the off-ball mover who created it (csPlayerId populated on only 2 of 1,422 events). So validating a receiver-side metric (SAR, SMS, TURN) against createsSpace is only a team/possession-level co-check; the true receiver labels are betterOptionPlayerId (n=518) and the movement/position grades (n=368/1,300) — small, sparse, and selection-biased to on-camera high-leverage moments.

Deduction-only grades cannot rank positive skill: movementGrade is ~71% negative and positionGrade ~99% negative, so 'best' means 'least-bad.' They validate that bad movement is penalized but cannot directly confirm that high SAR corresponds to GOOD movement — only that it avoids graded errors.

xG is noisy at the unit we'd most like to use: per-team-match grounding is weak (rho 0.33, out-of-fold 0.27) because of ~10-25 shots/match; grounding only holds at the team-tournament aggregate (rho 0.885 totals, but partly games-played-inflated; 0.45 per-match-rate is the cleaner, merely-moderate figure). Offensive chemistry beyond talent is directionally positive but underpowered and talent-entangled (mean_aw_joi90 partial +0.25, 90% CI [-0.13,+0.54] spans 0 at n=30); only the DEFENSIVE chemistry claim (n_strong_def partial -0.355, CI excludes 0) is firm. Do not overclaim the offensive side.

Geometric line-breaks diverge sharply from the human tag: only ~30% of geometric LB>=3 passes are PFF-tagged and ~21% of PFF-tagged passes reach LB>=3, and the metric is inflated for deep-buildup CBs/#6s threading past a high press — so the Stones/Modric/Rodri leaderboard partly measures a geometry-only construct, not graded tactical line breaks. Present it as progression-by-corridor, not as 'beating a defensive line.'

Many composites (SAR, CoCE, P-OBSO, SMS) are designed and feasibility-checked but not yet fully built/validated end-to-end in-repo; the validation runs (precision/recall vs createsSpace, the betterOption join, the pressure-gate fit) are the actual work, and the published numbers must distinguish what is computed (xT-lift 0.714, next-receiver 0.70, rho 0.885, defensive chemistry -0.355) from what is proposed.

Transfer/generalization to 2026 is an assumption: the model is calibrated on 2022 broadcast tracking and one tournament's styles; teasing a 2026 forecast on the same calibrated instrument assumes stability of playing styles, tracking pipeline, and the imputation regime that may not hold.
